question_text:
Lemonade Change

At a lemonade stand, each lemonade costs $5. Customers are standing in a queue to buy from you and order one at a time (in the order specified by bills). Each customer will only buy one lemonade and pay with either a $5, $10, or $20 bill. You must provide the correct change to each customer so that the net transaction is that the customer pays $5.

Note that you do not have any change in hand at first.

Given an integer array bills where bills[i] is the bill the ith customer pays, return true if you can provide every customer with the correct change, or false otherwise.
input_format: A single line of space-separated integers (each is 5, 10, or 20) representing the bills array.
output_format: Print "true" if you can provide correct change for every customer, else "false".
explanation: We greedily track the count of $5 and $10 bills we have. For a $5 bill, we simply pocket it. For a $10 bill, we need to return one $5 bill. For a $20 bill, we prefer to return one $10 + one $5 (to conserve our precious $5 stock), and only if no $10 is available do we return three $5 bills. If at any point we cannot make change, we return false. This greedy strategy is optimal because $5 bills are the most versatile currency for making change, and preserving them maximizes our flexibility for future transactions. O(N) time, O(1) space.
